sections.instruction: Questions 21-25: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C. Questions 26-30: Choose FIVE answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-G, next to Questions 26-30.

questions.content: What point does Robert make about the 2013 study in Britain?
options: A. It focused more on packaging than wasted food.
B. It proved that households produced more waste than restaurants.
C. It included liquid waste as well as solid waste.

questions.content: The speakers agree that food waste reports should emphasise the connection between carbon dioxide emissions and
options: B. transport of food to landfill sites.

questions.content: Television programmes now tend to focus on
options: A. the nutritional value of food products.
B. the origin of food products.
C. the chemicals found in food products.

questions.content: For Anna, the most significant point about food waste is
Anna and Robert decide to begin their presentation by
options: C. showing images of wasted food.

sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context:
Kite making by the Maori people of New Zealand

Making and appearance of kites
• The priests who made the kites had rules for size and scale
• {{BLANK}} was not allowed during a kite’s preparation

Kites:
• Often represented a god, a bird or a {{BLANK}}
• Had frames that were decorated with grasses and {{BLANK}}
• Had a line of noisy {{BLANK}} attached to them.
• Could be triangular, rectangular or {{BLANK}} shaped.
• Had patterns made from clay mixed with {{BLANK}} oil.
• Sometimes had human head masks with {{BLANK}} and a tattoo.

Purpose and function of kites:
• A way of sending {{BLANK}} to the gods
• A way of telling other villages that a {{BLANK}} was necessary
• A means of {{BLANK}} if enemies were coming.
